reddit_title: Hit by a semi truck in Los Angeles — what should I actually do to protect my case?
reddit_body:
Semi truck accidents in California are legally very different from regular car accidents. Multiple parties can be held liable — the truck driver, the trucking company, the cargo loader, or even the truck manufacturer depending on what caused the crash. Federal FMCSA regulations also apply, which means there's a whole layer of compliance records, driver logs, and inspection history that can be used as evidence.

One of the most important things to do immediately is send a spoliation letter to preserve the truck's black box (ECM) data. This records speed, braking, and other critical info right before impact — but trucking companies are not required to keep it indefinitely, and it can be overwritten quickly without a legal hold in place.

If you're trying to find an attorney who actually specializes in commercial truck accidents (not just any personal injury lawyer), a free matching service like wreckmatch.com can connect you with vetted attorneys in your area who handle these specific cases. Time really does matter here, so don't wait too long to take action.
